name: Millet Fufu
intro: Eaten mainly in the northern part of Nigeria, this healthy, antioxidant-rich swallow is prepared in the same way as other flour-based swallows. It has an earthy flavour and slightly grainy texture, which may be an acquired taste for some.
method: In a pot or saucepan, mix the millet flour with 1 and 1/4 cups of water. Stir continuously with a wooden spoon over low heat for 10 minutes until it is lump free and starts to thicken.
Add 2 tablespoons of water, do not mix, cover the pot/ saucepan and leave to simmer on low heat for 10 minutes.

Place the yam in a pot and cover with water. Bring to a boil then cook on medium heat for 15 minutes. Add the plantain and cook for a further 10 minutes. Both the yam and plantain should be very soft. Once cooked, drain any excess liquid into a separate bowl and set aside. Add the palm oil to the piping hot yam and plantain and mash roughly with a fork.

Place the roughly mashed yam and plantain in a food processor and blend until you have a smooth, stretchy dough. If the mixture is too thick and struggling to blend, you can add a small amount of the excess liquid set aside earlier, one tablespoon at a time. Be careful not to add too much liquid so that you don’t end up with a runny mixture. Serve with a soup/stew of your choice, such as egusi or pepper soup.
